Office of Undergraduate Research

Undergraduate students who are interested in engaging in research should explore resources and opportunities provided by the Office of Undergraduate Research (OUR). Contact OUR at our.umd.edu.

University Libraries

lib.umd.edu

The University Libraries is here to help you accomplish your work faster, easier, and better. Find space to study and collaborate, get help with research from expert librarians, and access the technology, resources, and library materials you need to succeed academically. Visit lib.umd.edu for anytime/ anywhere access to collections and services. Subject specialist librarians are available for research assistance in every discipline.

McKeldin Library is the main library on campus with a wide variety of services, spaces, and collections to support student success and offers late night study for the majority of the academic year. Individual and group spaces are available throughout McKeldin, including bookable group study rooms, specialty meeting rooms, a Family Study Room, and a Reflection Space. The Terrapin Learning Commons (TLC), a

popular collaborative space on the library’s second floor, offers computers, scanners, printers, multimedia equipment, and other technology to use and borrow, including podcasting and legacy media digitization labs. The GIS and Data Services Lab offers consultations and computing resources for geospatial data, data science, and data visualization. Students can borrow textbooks for some of the university’s most popular courses through the Top Textbooks program from McKeldin’s first floor service desk as well as the STEM Library. McKeldin is also home to about 1.8 million books, millions of government documents, and the Adaptive Technology Lab, which provides accessibility and disability services.

Hornbake Library houses special collections and exhibitions related to the University of Maryland, the state of Maryland, literature and rare books, postwar Japan, and American history— especially as it relates to labor, mass media, and the performing arts. Special collections are non-circulating and can be accessed in-person by appointment in designated reading rooms, including the Maryland Room in Hornbake Library.

Four other UMD campus libraries include: the Architecture Library; Art Library; Michelle Smith Performing Arts Library (which houses the International Piano Archives at Maryland and Special Collections in Performing Arts); and the STEM Library, which houses the John and Stella Graves Makerspace that offers 3D printers, 3D scanners and more for students of any major to innovate and create.
